calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-16-2025 Off Calendar

Motion To Confirm Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Sep-04-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: REFEREE'S MOTION TO CONFIRM SALE OF PROPERTY.

Appearances:
Michael S. Pecherer (925.518.7076) Referee and moving party; Nicole C. Kelly of the Kelly Law Firm (415.552.0059) appearing via Zoom for plaintiff; No other appearances.

Mr. Pecherer informs the Court there is one overbid to the listed price for the sale of the property (108 Chatanooga, San Francisco) in the amount of $1,785,000 (by Liza Zahner with City Real Estate who is present in court). No other overbids are present. A deposit is to be made with Fidelity in Walnut Creek.

The Court grants the motion and will review the proposed orders (1) granting the motion to confirm the sale, and (2) authorizing the referee to sign on behalf of the sellers.

Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-16-2024 Continued To Aug-28-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

HEARING ON ORDER RE: ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE FILED ON MAY 2, 2024
Off Calendar May-15-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: In-Persn Appearances

Nicole Kelly Esq., The Kelly Law Firm, 415-552-0059, for plaintiff
Ernest Canindin, Defendant in Pro per

Hearing held. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

ORDER RE: ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE FILED ON MAY 2, 2024 - Writ of Possession is granted. The Court will issue a writ of possession.

Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-03-2023 Continued To May-29-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Writ Of Possession
Off Calendar Oct-16-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Nicole C. Kelly; The Kelly Law Firm; 415-552-0059; appearing as counsel for plaintiffs Bobby Cacanindin and Alma Corzon Canindin.

Plaintiffs contest the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF POSSESSION is DENIED. Pursuant to the Interlocutory Judgment referee has the power "to evict any person or person residing in the Real Property." Per CCP 873.070 referee is instructed to proceed with legally authorized eviction proceedings.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR #13714, apourtabib@gmail.com, 415-601-7054. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-27-2023 Continued To May-31-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Default Judgment At Prove Up Hearing [Code Civ. Proc. 1005 And Cal. Rules Of Ct. 3.1800(A)].
Off Calendar Apr-14-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment at prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 02:09:40 p.m. and concluding at 02:28:56 p.m.

Plaintiff Bobby Cacanindin (A.K.A. Bonifacio Cacanindin) Successor Trustees of the Juanita E. Canindin Living Trust represented by Nicole C. Kelly, Esq.
Defendant Ernest Canindin not appearing.

BOBBY CACANINDIN and ALMA CANINDIN are sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Court confers with Plaintiff's Counsel re: distribution and division of property.
Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.

Court and Plaintiff's Counsel finalize proposed judgment.
BOBBY CACANINDIN and ALMA CANINDIN are examined by the Court. Witness attests that statements made by its counsel are true and correct.

The matter is submitted.
Proposed Interlocutory Default Judgment of Partition is signed as modified, filed, and served in open court.
